# Shuffle

> Shuffle is a free, open source, GPU-rendered file manager for macOS — a fast, native alternative to Finder. Built in Rust with the GPUI framework and rendered with Metal, it stays instant even in folders with 100,000+ items, and adds power-user tools like dual panes, tabs, a millisecond command palette, terminal mode, live previews, and deep theming. Tuned for Apple Silicon; runs on macOS 12+.

## Key facts

- **Name:** Shuffle
- **Category:** File manager / file explorer for macOS (Finder alternative)
- **Price:** Free and open source (MIT license). A one-time purchase for extra power-user features is planned — no subscriptions, ever.
- **Platform:** macOS 12 or later. Tuned for Apple Silicon (M-series); runs on modern Intel Macs (Intel builds from source).
- **Built with:** Rust and GPUI (the GPU UI framework behind the Zed editor); GPU-rendered with Metal.
- **Inspiration:** File Pilot on Windows — an open source, GPU-rendered file manager focused on performance and power-user features.
- **Author:** Jaime Guzman
- **Source code:** https://github.com/WizenPainter/shuffle
- **Download (always latest release):** https://github.com/WizenPainter/shuffle/releases/latest/download/Shuffle.dmg

## Why choose Shuffle

macOS file managers are either too limited or too slow. Shuffle is a single, small, native app that stays instant no matter how big the directory or how fast you move, and gives power users real tools instead of getting in the way. No Electron, no jank.

## Features

- **Dual panes / split canvas:** Drag a tab to the edge to split the window into two side-by-side panes, each with its own tabs, history, and filter; a draggable divider resizes them.
- **Tabs:** Open with Cmd+T, smoothly drag to reorder, and drag tabs between panes.
- **Command palette (Cmd+P):** Millisecond fuzzy search over your home directory with typo tolerance, live path browsing, and quick commands.
- **Terminal mode:** Optional command bar to move through the explorer like a shell — cd navigates, Tab autocompletes.
- **Preview & Information:** Single-click to QuickLook files (images, PDFs, docs) and inspect details in a side inspector.
- **In-place filter (/):** Instantly narrow the current folder, typo tolerant.
- **Cloud & servers:** Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, and iCloud appear automatically, alongside mounted volumes and a Connect to Server dialog.
- **Deep theming:** Dozens of presets (Catppuccin, Dracula, Nord, Gruvbox, Solarized, Tokyo Night) plus per-color customization, applied live.

## Keyboard shortcuts

- Cmd+P — Command palette / fuzzy find
- / — Filter the current directory
- Cmd+T — New tab
- Cmd+W — Close tab
- Cmd+, — Settings

## Installation

1. Download Shuffle.dmg (latest release) from https://github.com/WizenPainter/shuffle/releases/latest
2. Open the disk image and drag Shuffle into your Applications folder.
3. Developers can build from source: clone https://github.com/WizenPainter/shuffle, then run `cargo build --release`.

- The best Finder alternatives for macOS in 2026, compared: https://shuffleapp.co/blog/best-finder-alternatives-macos
- Why is Finder so slow? And what you can actually do about it: https://shuffleapp.co/blog/why-is-finder-slow
- What makes a file manager fast?: https://shuffleapp.co/blog/what-makes-a-file-manager-fast
- From DIRMAINT to Finder: a brief history of the file manager: https://shuffleapp.co/blog/history-of-the-file-manager
- The four archetypes of file managers: https://shuffleapp.co/blog/file-manager-archetypes
- 8.3 filenames and cluster chains: how the DOS FAT file system worked: https://shuffleapp.co/blog/how-the-dos-fat-file-system-worked
- APFS explained: what your Mac's file system does under the hood: https://shuffleapp.co/blog/apfs-how-your-macs-file-system-works
- How fuzzy search works: typo-tolerant matching explained: https://shuffleapp.co/blog/how-fuzzy-search-works
- Dual-pane file managers: why a 1986 layout still wins: https://shuffleapp.co/blog/dual-pane-file-managers
- Building a GPU-rendered file manager for macOS in Rust with GPUI: https://shuffleapp.co/blog/building-shuffle-rust-gpui
